file_insights.purpose: Core CASL-based permission service implementing RBAC and ABAC access control
file_insights.architecture_pattern: Service injectable with CASL ability builder pattern

patterns_discovered.description: Uses strict TypeScript types for actions and subjects instead of strings, enabling type-safe permission checks

patterns_discovered.pattern: Plan tier hierarchy with numeric levels
patterns_discovered.description: Maps plan tiers to numeric levels for easy comparison and feature gating
Separates platform-level roles (super_admin, admin, sales, support) from organization-level roles (owner, manager, staff, cashier, viewer)
Explicit mapping of each role to its permitted actions across resources
Uses CASL's AbilityBuilder pattern for flexible permission rule composition
Central registry mapping features to minimum required plan tiers
Extensive inline documentation with examples for all public methods and types

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Organization context optional but required for org roles
gotchas_discovered.description: defineAbilitiesFor accepts optional organization parameter, but organization roles cannot function without it. Returns empty ability if org is missing for non-platform roles.
line_indicators: Early return with empty build() if !organization for non-platform roles
gotchas_discovered.impact: Could silently grant zero permissions if organization context is forgotten in method calls

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Plan tier parameter is optional but influences permissions
gotchas_discovered.description: planTier parameter accepts null/undefined but this bypasses plan-based restrictions entirely
line_indicators: getPlanLevel method returns 0 for null/undefined tiers
gotchas_discovered.impact: Missing plan tier could grant unintended permissions for features gated by plan level

gotchas_discovered.description: Feature-to-plan mappings are hardcoded in getMinimumPlanForFeature method, making them not configurable at runtime
gotchas_discovered.impact: Plan feature restrictions cannot be changed without code deployment

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Role permissions are string-based, not type-safe
gotchas_discovered.description: getPermissionsForRole returns string arrays (e.g., 'menu.create') which are not validated against PermissionAction/PermissionSubject types
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential for typos or invalid permission strings that won't be caught at compile time

gotchas_discovered.description: getPermissionsForRole and role-based ability checks silently return zero permissions for unrecognized roles
gotchas_discovered.impact: Typos in role names result in silent permission denial rather than explicit error

approach_outcome.deliverable: Complete permission service with 975 lines of well-documented TypeScript
approach_outcome.commit_message: auto-claude: subtask-6-2 - Create CASL Permission service
approach_outcome.implementation_scope: Single new file containing comprehensive permission management system
design_decisions: Used @injectable decorator for dependency injection integration
Implemented CASL MongoAbility for flexible permission rules
Separated platform and organization roles into distinct systems
Created plan tier hierarchy for feature gating
Included extensive role-to-permission mappings for all 8 roles
Provided utility methods for plan comparison and feature restrictions
approach_outcome.completeness: Appears truncated in diff output (29633 chars), but core implementation present

recommendations.recommendation: Convert role permission strings in getPermissionsForRole to strongly-typed action/subject pairs
recommendations.rationale: Current string format ('menu.create') is not validated against PermissionAction/PermissionSubject types, allowing typos
recommendations.suggested_approach: Replace string array return with RolePermission[] interface array, ensuring compile-time validation

recommendations.recommendation: Add explicit validation for missing organization context in organization-role users
recommendations.rationale: Silent permission denial when organization is missing could be hard to debug in production
recommendations.suggested_approach: Throw AppException.badRequest if organization is required but missing, rather than returning empty ability

recommendations.recommendation: Externalize feature-to-plan mappings from hardcoded dictionary
recommendations.rationale: Plan feature restrictions are currently hardcoded, requiring code changes for runtime updates
recommendations.suggested_approach: Load featurePlanMap from configuration file or environment, or make it injectable from a FeatureConfigService

recommendations.recommendation: Add validation and logging for unrecognized roles
recommendations.rationale: Unknown roles silently result in zero permissions, making debugging difficult
recommendations.suggested_approach: Log warnings when encountering unknown roles, optionally throw error based on environment

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive test suite covering all role combinations and plan tier scenarios
recommendations.rationale: 975-line permission service is critical for security; needs thorough test coverage
recommendations.suggested_approach: Unit tests for each role's ability matrix, integration tests for combined conditions (role + plan + org)

recommendations.recommendation: Add security considerations section to module documentation
recommendations.rationale: Existing JSDoc is excellent but lacks security implications discussion
recommendations.suggested_approach: Add section covering plan-based restriction bypass risks and org context requirements
